description_long: This object is a delicate, wispy flower with extremely thin, filament-like stems and petals that curve and loop in an airy, almost abstract arrangement. The structure is sparse, with most of its volume consisting of open space, and the petals form large, looping arcs that intersect at a central vertical axis. At the tips of some filaments, there are small, coiled or tufted structures that may represent flower buds or seed heads. The coloration is a pale, muted brownish-pink, giving the flower a dried or ethereal appearance. There are no visible broad leaves, and the overall form is highly linear and open, resembling the dried remains of a wildflower or ornamental grass. The flower appears fragile and lightweight, suitable for decorative use in urban parks or naturalized landscaping.
description: A wispy, airy flower with thin, looping filaments and a pale brownish-pink color, featuring coiled tips and a sparse, delicate structure.
description_view_0: From this view, the looping filaments form a butterfly-like shape with a central vertical axis and visible coiled tip on the upper left.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the thinness and curvature of the filaments, with two prominent tips extending outward.
description_view_2: The filaments appear more symmetrical, with the coiled tip now on the upper right and the loops forming a mirrored pattern.
description_view_3: A narrow side profile showing the elongated, linear nature of the filaments and the sparse, open structure.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a loose, radial configuration with long, filamentous extensions that loop and cross each other, lacking clear symmetry or whorled layering.
